title: Chromolaena mattogrossensis
intro: Chromolaena mattogrossensis, also known as 'erva-baleeira', is a plant species within the Asteraceae family, native to the Pantanal region of South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Chromolaena mattogrossensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Chromolaena mattogrossensis include teas, infusions, and extracts, often used in folk medicine to alleviate various health conditions.
taxonomy: Chromolaena mattogrossensis belongs to the family Asteraceae. This plant is a species of flowering plant in the genus Chromolaena. Chromolaena mattogrossensis is a type of weed native to the tropical regions of South America. It is closely related to other species within the genus Chromolaena, which also includes Chromolaena odorata. Further classification of Chromolaena mattogrossensis can be determined at the infraspecific level.
uses: Chromolaena mattogrossensis has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its purported anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's leaves and stems are said to be effective in treating various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions. In some regions, Chromolaena mattogrossensis is also used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Additionally, its extracts are believed to possess antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. Further research is necessary to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential applications.
